channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US climber Tyler Andrews sets new Everest speed record with oxygen support
item.description: According to reports, US climber Tyler Andrews has set a new speed record on Mount Everest after reaching the summit of the world’s highest mountain in 9 hours and 55 minutes. The 36-year-old climber ...

item.title: Street Fighter 6 Sets Server Maintenance for Ingrid Release and Season 3 Conclusion Update
item.description: Street Fighter 6 is preparing for its biggest content drop since launching in 2023 as Season 3 comes to a close. Capcom has announced server maintenance on May 27, 2026, starting at 7 p.m. PDT and exp...

item.title: Limpopo Police Warn of Ongoing Violence Despite Crime Decline, Amid Separate Cases of Police Misconduct and Court Delays
item.description: Limpopo Police Commissioner Lieutenant General Thembi Hadebe has raised concerns about persistent violent crime in the province, even though official figures show a decline in serious offences such as...

item.title: Concerns raised over reshuffle in King Misuzulu’s royal household following dismissal of senior aides
item.description: Tensions within the AmaZulu royal household have intensified following the dismissal of two senior officials in King Misuzulu kaZwelithini’s inner circle. Inkosi Malusi Zondi, who served as royal chan...

item.title: US military warns adversaries may be using commercial location data to track troops
item.description: US military officials have raised concerns that American forces deployed in conflict zones are being tracked and potentially targeted using commercially available location data. According to a letter ...

item.title: Stakeholders call for reforms to improve Tanzania’s public education system
item.description: Tanzania is currently facing serious challenges in its public education sector, with growing concern from experts, policymakers, and education stakeholders about widening gaps in access, quality, and ...

item.title: Chandrayaan-2 Identifies Potential Subsurface Ice in Moon’s South Polar Craters
item.description: Scientists analysing data from India’s Chandrayaan-2 mission have discovered strong evidence suggesting the presence of subsurface ice near the Moon’s South Polar Region. This breakthrough comes from ...

item.title: CBI Arrests Retired Judge Giribala Singh in Connection with Twisha Sharma's Death
item.description: On Thursday afternoon, the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) arrested retired judge Giribala Singh in connection with the death of her daughter-in-law, Twisha Sharma. This development came after t...

item.title: Lululemon and founder Chip Wilson reach agreement ending proxy dispute
item.description: Lululemon Athletica and its founder Chip Wilson have reached a truce after a prolonged and very public dispute over the company’s direction and board composition. The agreement marks a pause in a bitt...

item.title: CIDG files obstruction of justice complaint over alleged aid in Sen. Dela Rosa's escape
item.description: The Philippine National Police–Criminal Investigation and Detection Group (PNP-CIDG) has formally filed a complaint for obstruction of justice against Senator Robin Padilla and several others before t...

item.title: Australian saturation divers describe life in pressurised underwater chambers working in extreme conditions
item.description: The article explores the extraordinary and highly specialised world of saturation diving through the experiences of Australians Reg Hyde and Chris Eckert. These commercial divers spend weeks at a time...

item.title: Aljon Mendoza Reflects on His Departure from Star Magic
item.description: Aljon Mendoza, a well-known Filipino actor, recently shared his thoughts and feelings after parting ways with Star Magic, the talent management arm of ABS-CBN. In an emotional statement, Mendoza admit...

item.title: Knife attack at Winterthur train station injures three; suspect arrested
item.description: A knife attack at a train station in the Swiss city of Winterthur, near Zurich, left three people injured early Thursday morning, according to local police and Reuters reporting. Authorities said the ...

item.title: Government launches full investigation into Utumishi Girls Academy fire that killed 16 students
item.description: The Government of Kenya has announced a full and comprehensive investigation into the tragic fire that broke out at Utumishi Girls Academy Senior School in Gilgil, Nakuru County, which has so far clai...

item.title: Tanzania Health Budget 2026/27 Allocates TZS 1.8 Trillion to Boost Local Drug Production and Health System Expansion
item.description: Tanzania’s Ministry of Health has presented a TZS 1.8 trillion budget for the 2026/27 financial year, focusing strongly on expanding healthcare infrastructure, improving service delivery, and strength...

item.title: Deputy PM Ishaq Dar arrives in New York for UNSC debate on global peace and multilateralism
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar has arrived in New York on a three-day official visit to participate in a high-level United Nations Security Council (UNSC) open debate focused on ...

item.title: Calls for a Reset of the NACC Following Commissioner Resignation and Operational Concerns
item.description: The National Anti-Corruption Commission (NACC), established by the Labor government in July 2023, has faced a tumultuous start, culminating in the resignation of its first commissioner, Paul Brereton,...

item.title: ICC sets Duterte trial on November 30, 2026 during first status conference
item.description: Nagdaos ang International Criminal Court (ICC) ng unang status conference para sa kaso ni dating Pangulong Rodrigo Duterte, kung saan tinalakay ang mahahalagang pre-trial matters tulad ng iskedyul ng ...

item.title: ASIO warns MPs over discussing sensitive information in connected cars
item.description: Australia’s domestic intelligence agency, ASIO, has issued a security warning to politicians and public servants, advising them against discussing sensitive or classified matters inside vehicles. The ...

item.title: Canada advances negotiations with Saab for early-warning aircraft as defence procurement strategy shifts
item.description: Canada is moving forward in negotiations with Swedish defence company Saab to acquire its GlobalEye airborne early-warning and control system, marking a significant step in Ottawa’s efforts to reduce ...
